Tool Call #421 Agent tooluse_oBuW5eQ3DVSM3ubo7bt9xr 13:47:34
Agent input={"description":"Round 2 security and architecture review","prompt":"You are a senior security and architecture reviewer. This is the SECOND independent review of a sql.js → bett...
Show input payload 
{
  "description": "Round 2 security and architecture review",
  "prompt": "You are a senior security and architecture reviewer. This is the SECOND independent review of a sql.js → better-sqlite3 migration in a TypeScript monorepo.\n\nA first reviewer already found these issues:\n- CRITICAL: searchTranscripts opens DB readonly then calls CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE (write op → throws)\n- CRITICAL: transcripts_fts_segdir check is fragile and unreliable\n- HIGH: db.prepare(UPSERT_SQL).run(...params) — should pass array directly, not spread\n- HIGH: findLatestSession returns null instead of JSONL fallback when DB has no matching row\n- HIGH: No busy_timeout set — concurrent writers get SQLITE_BUSY immediately\n- MEDIUM: Full FTS rebuild on every single upsert (O(n²) for bulk imports)\n- MEDIUM: Prepared statements not reused across calls\n- MEDIUM: sanitizeFtsQuery allows single quotes through — unbalanced quote causes FTS parse error\n- LOW: existsSync + mkdirSync TOCTOU race (use mkdirSync unconditionally)\n- LOW: No path validation on dbPath\n\nYour job: Do a SECOND pass focusing on things the first reviewer may have MISSED. Look for:\n1. TypeScript type safety issues\n2. Module-level state / singleton concerns\n3. Compatibility issues (Node 22/24, Windows paths)\n4. Test coverage gaps in the new db.test.ts\n5. Any issues with the FTS4 content table approach specifically\n6. The `recall.ts` change — is the sync conversion complete and correct?\n7. Anything in import.ts that could break with the sync change\n\nHere is the full code:\n\n### db.ts\n```typescript\nimport Database from 'better-sqlite3'\nimport { existsSync, mkdirSync } from 'node:fs'\nimport { dirname } from 'node:path'\nimport type { ManifestEntry } from '@openmnemo/types'\n\nfunction openDb(dbPath: string): InstanceType<typeof Database> {\n  const dir = dirname(dbPath)\n  if (!existsSync(dir)) mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true })\n  const db = new Database(dbPath)\n  db.pragma('journal_mode = WAL')\n  return db\n}\n\nexport function searchTranscripts(dbPath: string, query: string, limit = 20): SearchResult[] {\n  const sanitized = sanitizeFtsQuery(query)\n  if (!sanitized) return []\n  if (!existsSync(dbPath)) return []\n  const db = new Database(dbPath, { readonly: true })\n  try {\n    db.exec(CREATE_FTS_SQL)  // ← CRITICAL: write on readonly\n    const segCount = (db.prepare('SELECT COUNT(*) as cnt FROM transcripts_fts_segdir').get() as { cnt: number }).cnt\n    if (segCount === 0) db.exec(REBUILD_FTS_SQL)\n    return db.prepare(SEARCH_SQL).all(sanitized, limit) as SearchResult[]\n  } finally {\n    db.close()\n  }\n}\n\nexport function upsertSearchIndex(dbPath: string, manifest: ManifestEntry): void {\n  const db = openDb(dbPath)\n  try {\n    db.exec(CREATE_TABLE_SQL)\n    const params = ALL_COLUMNS.map((col) => {\n      const value = (manifest as Record<string, unknown>)[col]\n      if (value === undefined || value === null) return ''\n      return typeof value === 'number' ? value : String(value)\n    })\n    db.prepare(UPSERT_SQL).run(...params)\n    db.exec(CREATE_FTS_SQL)\n    db.exec(REBUILD_FTS_SQL)\n  } finally {\n    db.close()\n  }\n}\n```\n\n### recall.ts (changed section)\n```typescript\n// recall() is still async (calls await syncCurrentProject which is async)\nexport async function recall(...): Promise<RecallResult> {\n  const imported = await syncCurrentProject(...)\n  const session = findLatestSession(...)  // now sync, no await\n  // ...\n}\n\n// findLatestSession is now sync\nexport function findLatestSession(...): Record<string, unknown> | null {\n  if (!existsSync(dbPath)) return findLatestFromJsonl(...)\n  try {\n    const db = new Database(dbPath, { readonly: true })\n    try {\n      const rows = db.prepare('SELECT * FROM transcripts WHERE started_at < ? ORDER BY started_at DESC LIMIT 20').all(activationTime) as Record<string, unknown>[]\n      for (const row of rows) {\n        if (cwdMatches(cwd, resolvedRoot) || project === repoSlug) return row\n      }\n    } finally {\n      db.close()\n    }\n  } catch {\n    return findLatestFromJsonl(...)\n  }\n  return null  // ← no JSONL fallback here\n}\n```\n\n### import.ts change\n```typescript\n// Before: await upsertSearchIndex(globalDbPath, manifest)\n// After:\nupsertSearchIndex(globalDbPath, manifest)  // sync, no await\n// importTranscript() is still async (other awaits remain)\n```\n\nRate each NEW issue as CRITICAL / HIGH / MEDIUM / LOW. Do NOT repeat issues already found by reviewer 1. Focus only on what was missed.",
  "subagent_type": "general-purpose"
}

Agent output=[{"text":"Here is the full second-pass review. All issues below are distinct from the first reviewer's findings.\n\n---\n\n## Second-Pass Review: sql.js → better-sqlite3 Migrati...
Show output payload 
[
  {
    "text": "Here is the full second-pass review. All issues below are distinct from the first reviewer's findings.\n\n---\n\n## Second-Pass Review: sql.js → better-sqlite3 Migration\n\n---\n\n### CRITICAL\n\n**C1. `db.prepare(UPSERT_SQL).run(...params)` — spread of 18 args hits V8 argument limit on Node 22/24**\n\n`better-sqlite3`'s `Statement.run()` accepts either a rest spread or a single array. Spreading 18 values is fine today, but the real problem is that `run(...params)` where `params` is `unknown[]` is not type-safe — TypeScript infers the spread as `unknown[]` and the call compiles only because `run` is typed as `run(...bindParameters: BindingDictionary): RunResult`. The actual runtime type of each element is `string | number`, but TypeScript cannot verify that because `params` is built via a `.map()` returning `string | number | ''`. If `ManifestEntry` ever gains a field whose value survives the `typeof value === 'number'` branch as something else (e.g. a boolean from a future schema change), it silently coerces to a string — no compile error, no runtime error, wrong data. The fix is `db.prepare(UPSERT_SQL).run(params)` (single array argument), which is also what reviewer 1 flagged for a different reason. Noting here because the type-safety angle was missed.\n\n**C2. FTS4 content table `docid = t.rowid` join is broken after any DELETE or VACUUM**\n\n`SEARCH_SQL` joins on `transcripts_fts.docid = t.rowid`. With a content table (`content='transcripts'`), FTS4 stores the rowid of the source row at index time. If any row in `transcripts` is deleted and re-inserted (which the upsert does via `ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE` — SQLite's `UPDATE` path preserves rowid, but a future `DELETE` + `INSERT` would not), or if `VACUUM` is run (which renumbers rowids in non-WAL mode), the docid→rowid mapping silently diverges. The `REBUILD_FTS_SQL` re-syncs it, but only if called. Since `searchTranscripts` only rebuilds when `segdir` count is zero (already flagged as fragile by reviewer 1), a post-vacuum search returns rows joined to the wrong transcript. This is a data-correctness bug, not just a reliability issue.\n\n---\n\n### HIGH\n\n**H1. `findLatestSession` — `resolve(cwd)` called with an arbitrary DB string value on line 226 of recall.ts**\n\n```typescript\nconst cwdResolved = toPosixPath(resolve(cwd)).toLowerCase()\n```\n\n`cwd` here comes directly from a SQLite row (`String(row['cwd'] ?? '')`). `resolve()` on Windows will interpret a value like `C:\\Users\\...` correctly, but a value like `\\\\server\\share\\path` (UNC path stored by a Windows user) causes `resolve()` to return an unexpected result. More critically, if the DB row contains a relative path (e.g. `project`), `resolve()` silently anchors it to the current process working directory, making `cwdMatches` return a false positive. There is no validation that the stored `cwd` is absolute before calling `resolve()`.\n\n**H2. `import.ts` line 105 — fire-and-forget `upsertSearchIndex` swallows all errors silently**\n\n```typescript\nupsertSearchIndex(globalDbPath, manifest)  // sync, no await\n```\n\n`upsertSearchIndex` is now synchronous and can throw (e.g. disk full, permission denied, better-sqlite3 native binding not found). Previously it was `await`-ed inside a `try/catch` in the caller. Now the call is bare — any throw propagates up through `importTranscript` and will cause the entire import to fail, including the already-written manifest JSON and clean markdown. The caller (`syncCurrentProject` in recall.ts) catches per-transcript errors and continues, so the import count will be wrong (session counted as failed when files were actually written). The fix is wrapping the call in a try/catch and logging rather than throwing.\n\n**H3. `ManifestEntry` fields not in `ALL_COLUMNS` are silently dropped — type system gives no warning**\n\n`ALL_COLUMNS` is missing `cleaning_mode`, `repo_mirror_enabled`, and `global_manifest_path` (present in `ManifestEntry` per import.ts lines 85–87). The `params` map casts `manifest` to `Record<string, unknown>` and iterates only `ALL_COLUMNS`, so those three fields are never written to SQLite. `findLatestSession` returns a raw DB row, and callers in recall.ts do `String(session['global_clean_path'] ?? '')` — but `global_clean_path` IS in `ALL_COLUMNS`, so that specific one is fine. However, any future caller that reads `cleaning_mode` or `repo_mirror_enabled` from the returned row will silently get `undefined`. TypeScript cannot catch this because the return type is `Record<string, unknown>`. The schema and `ALL_COLUMNS` are not derived from the same source of truth.\n\n---\n\n### MEDIUM\n\n**M1. Module-level `ALL_COLUMNS` is a `readonly` tuple of string literals — but `UPSERT_SQL` is built at module load time with no schema validation**\n\n`UPSERT_SQL` is constructed by joining `ALL_COLUMNS` at module evaluation time. If `ManifestEntry` (from `@openmnemo/types`) is updated to add or rename a column, `ALL_COLUMNS` must be manually kept in sync. There is no compile-time check that `ALL_COLUMNS[number]` is a subset of `keyof ManifestEntry`. The cast `(manifest as Record<string, unknown>)[col]` suppresses the type error. A `satisfies` constraint or a mapped type check would catch drift at compile time.\n\n**M2. `searchTranscripts` test suite never tests against a DB that was NOT populated by `upsertSearchIndex` in the same test**\n\nAll `searchTranscripts` tests call `upsertSearchIndex` first in the same process. This means the FTS table always exists and is always populated before search runs. The critical path — a DB that has the `transcripts` table but no `transcripts_fts` table (legacy DB, the stated use case in the comment on line 153 of db.ts) — is never exercised. The `CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE IF NOT EXISTS` + readonly bug (already flagged by reviewer 1) would be caught by such a test.\n\n**M3. `sanitizeFtsQuery` single-quote passthrough — test exists but tests the wrong thing**\n\nThe test at line 263 checks that `'\"auth* (bug)-'` becomes `'auth bug'` — the double-quote is stripped but the single quote in the input is not present in that test string. There is no test for input like `it's` or `O'Brien` which produces `it's` or `O'Brien` — a string with an unbalanced single quote. FTS4 treats single quotes as string delimiters in MATCH expressions. `MATCH 'it''s'` is valid SQL (escaped), but `MATCH it's` (after sanitization strips nothing) is a parse error. The test suite does not cover this case, so the known bug from reviewer 1 has no regression test.\n\n**M4. `findLatestSession` — `activationTime` comparison uses lexicographic string compare on ISO timestamps**\n\n```typescript\ndb.prepare('SELECT * FROM transcripts WHERE started_at < ? ORDER BY started_at DESC LIMIT 20').all(activationTime)\n```\n\nThis works correctly only if `started_at` values are consistently formatted ISO 8601 with the same precision and timezone offset. If any transcript has `started_at` stored as a Unix timestamp integer, a locale-formatted date, or an ISO string with a non-UTC offset (e.g. `2024-06-01T08:00:00+08:00`), the lexicographic comparison gives wrong results. The JSONL fallback (line 200) has the same issue: `if (started >= activationTime) continue`. There is no normalization or SQLite `datetime()` coercion applied.\n\n---\n\n### LOW\n\n**L1. `better-sqlite3` import in `recall.ts` is a direct `import Database from 'better-sqlite3'` — duplicates the import already in `db.ts`**\n\n`recall.ts` imports `better-sqlite3` directly for `findLatestSession`, while `db.ts` also imports it. This means two separate modules both hold a reference to the native addon. On most platforms this is fine (Node caches native modules), but it creates a maintenance coupling: if the import is ever aliased or mocked in tests, both files must be updated. `findLatestSession` should use a thin wrapper from `db.ts` (e.g. a `queryTranscripts` export) rather than opening SQLite directly.\n\n**L2. `db.test.ts` — `makeManifest` uses hardcoded Unix paths for `cwd`, `global_raw_path`, etc.**\n\n```typescript\ncwd: '/home/user/project',\nglobal_raw_path: '/global/raw/file.jsonl',\n```\n\nTests run on Windows (the repo's platform per env). These paths are stored as-is in SQLite. `cwdMatches` in recall.ts calls `toPosixPath(resolve(cwd)).toLowerCase()` — `resolve('/home/user/project')` on Windows returns `C:\\home\\user\\project` (anchored to current drive), not `/home/user/project`. Any test that exercises the full `findLatestSession` → `cwdMatches` path with these fixtures will produce false negatives on Windows. The test suite currently does not test `findLatestSession` directly, so this is latent, but it will bite when those tests are added.\n\n**L3. `recall.ts` — `syncCurrentProject` calls `importTranscript` which calls `upsertSearchIndex` synchronously, but `syncCurrentProject` is still `async` and its callers `await` it**\n\nThis is correct and not a bug, but the async boundary is now misleading: `syncCurrentProject` is `async` only because it `await`s `importTranscript`, which is `async` only because of other I/O inside it (not because of the DB call). If a future refactor removes those other awaits, the function silently becomes a synchronous operation wrapped in a resolved Promise, which is fine but wastes a microtask tick per transcript. Worth a comment.\n\n**L4. `db.test.ts` — no test for `upsertSearchIndex` with a manifest that has `undefined` or `null` fields**\n\nThe `params` map in `upsertSearchIndex` converts `undefined`/`null` to `''`. There is no test that passes a manifest with missing optional fields (e.g. `branch: undefined`) and verifies the stored value is `''` rather than the string `'undefined'`. The `typeof value === 'number'` branch also means a `0` message count is stored correctly, but there is no test for `message_count: 0` specifically.\n\n---\n\n### Summary table\n\n| ID | Severity | Area | One-line description |\n|----|----------|------|----------------------|\n| C1 | CRITICAL | Type safety | `run(...params)` not type-safe; boolean/object fields coerce silently |\n| C2 | CRITICAL | FTS4 correctness | `docid = rowid` join breaks after DELETE or VACUUM |\n| H1 | HIGH | Windows compat | `resolve(cwd)` on relative/UNC DB values gives false positives |\n| H2 | HIGH | Error handling | Bare `upsertSearchIndex()` call in import.ts throws through and corrupts import count |\n| H3 | HIGH | Type safety | `ALL_COLUMNS` missing 3 `ManifestEntry` fields; silently dropped, no compile error |\n| M1 | MEDIUM | Type safety | `ALL_COLUMNS` not constrained to `keyof ManifestEntry`; schema drift undetected |\n| M2 | MEDIUM | Test coverage | No test for legacy DB (transcripts table exists, FTS table missing) |\n| M3 | MEDIUM | Test coverage | Single-quote passthrough has no regression test |\n| M4 | MEDIUM | Correctness | ISO timestamp lexicographic compare breaks on non-UTC or non-ISO stored values |\n| L1 | LOW | Architecture | `recall.ts` imports `better-sqlite3` directly, duplicating `db.ts` coupling |\n| L2 | LOW | Windows compat | Test fixtures use Unix paths; `cwdMatches` will fail on Windows when exercised |\n| L3 | LOW | Clarity | `syncCurrentProject` async boundary is misleading post-sync refactor |\n| L4 | LOW | Test coverage | No test for null/undefined manifest fields or `message_count: 0` |",
    "type": "text"
  },
  {
    "type": "text",

  }
]
assistant #423 13:49:28

两轮 review 完成。汇总所有需要修复的问题，按优先级排列：

CRITICAL（必须修复）
1. searchTranscripts 用 readonly 打开 DB 却调用 CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE（写操作）→ 会 throw
2. transcripts_fts_segdir 检查不可靠且在 readonly 连接上可能失败
3. db.prepare(UPSERT_SQL).run(...params) 应改为 .run(params)（传数组而非展开）
4. FTS4 docid = t.rowid join 在 DELETE/VACUUM 后会错位

HIGH（应修复）
5. findLatestSession 无匹配时返回 null 而非 fallback 到 JSONL
6. import.ts 裸调用 upsertSearchIndex() 不捕获异常，会导致 import 计数错误
7. ALL_COLUMNS 缺少 cleaning_mode、repo_mirror_enabled、global_manifest_path 三个字段
8. 并发写入无 busy_timeout，立即 SQLITE_BUSY

MEDIUM
9. 每次 upsert 都全量 rebuild FTS（O(n²)）
10. sanitizeFtsQuery 放过单引号，FTS4 MATCH 会 parse error
11. ALL_COLUMNS 未约束为 keyof ManifestEntry，schema 漂移无编译报错

现在开始修复，从 CRITICAL 开始：

Show output payload 
1→/**
     2→ * Transcript search index — SQLite upsert for the global transcript catalog.
     3→ *
     4→ * Uses better-sqlite3 (native binding) for synchronous SQLite access.
     5→ * Supports FTS4 full-text search on title, cwd, branch columns.
     6→ */
     7→
     8→import Database from 'better-sqlite3'
     9→import { existsSync, mkdirSync } from 'node:fs'
    10→import { dirname } from 'node:path'
    11→import type { ManifestEntry } from '@openmnemo/types'
    12→
    13→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    14→// Column definitions
    15→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    16→
    17→const PK_COLUMNS = [
    18→  'client',
    19→  'project',
    20→  'session_id',
    21→  'raw_sha256',
    22→] as const
    23→
    24→const DATA_COLUMNS = [
    25→  'title',
    26→  'started_at',
    27→  'imported_at',
    28→  'cwd',
    29→  'branch',
    30→  'raw_source_path',
    31→  'raw_upload_permission',
    32→  'global_raw_path',
    33→  'global_clean_path',
    34→  'repo_raw_path',
    35→  'repo_clean_path',
    36→  'repo_manifest_path',
    37→  'message_count',
    38→  'tool_event_count',
    39→] as const
    40→
    41→const ALL_COLUMNS = [...PK_COLUMNS, ...DATA_COLUMNS] as const
    42→
    43→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    44→// SQL statements
    45→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    46→
    47→const CREATE_TABLE_SQL = `
    48→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS transcripts (
    49→    client              TEXT    NOT NULL,
    50→    project             TEXT    NOT NULL,
    51→    session_id          TEXT    NOT NULL,
    52→    raw_sha256          TEXT    NOT NULL,
    53→    title               TEXT    NOT NULL,
    54→    started_at          TEXT    NOT NULL,
    55→    imported_at         TEXT    NOT NULL,
    56→    cwd                 TEXT    NOT NULL,
    57→    branch              TEXT    NOT NULL,
    58→    raw_source_path     TEXT    NOT NULL,
    59→    raw_upload_permission TEXT  NOT NULL,
    60→    global_raw_path     TEXT    NOT NULL,
    61→    global_clean_path   TEXT    NOT NULL,
    62→    repo_raw_path       TEXT    NOT NULL,
    63→    repo_clean_path     TEXT    NOT NULL,
    64→    repo_manifest_path  TEXT    NOT NULL,
    65→    message_count       INTEGER NOT NULL,
    66→    tool_event_count    INTEGER NOT NULL,
    67→    PRIMARY KEY (client, project, session_id, raw_sha256)
    68→  )
    69→`
    70→
    71→const UPSERT_SQL = `
    72→  INSERT INTO transcripts (
    73→    ${ALL_COLUMNS.join(', ')}
    74→  ) VALUES (${ALL_COLUMNS.map(() => '?').join(', ')})
    75→  ON CONFLICT(${PK_COLUMNS.join(', ')}) DO UPDATE SET
    76→    ${DATA_COLUMNS.map((c) => `${c} = excluded.${c}`).join(',\n    ')}
    77→`
    78→
    79→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    80→// FTS4 full-text search
    81→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    82→
    83→const CREATE_FTS_SQL = `
    84→  CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE IF NOT EXISTS transcripts_fts USING fts4(
    85→    content='transcripts',
    86→    title, cwd, branch
    87→  )
    88→`
    89→
    90→const REBUILD_FTS_SQL = `
    91→  INSERT INTO transcripts_fts(transcripts_fts) VALUES('rebuild')
    92→`
    93→
    94→const SEARCH_SQL = `
    95→  SELECT t.client, t.project, t.session_id, t.title, t.cwd, t.branch, t.started_at
    96→  FROM transcripts_fts
    97→  JOIN transcripts t ON transcripts_fts.docid = t.rowid
    98→  WHERE transcripts_fts MATCH ?
    99→  ORDER BY t.started_at DESC
   100→  LIMIT ?
   101→`
   102→
   103→export interface SearchResult {
   104→  client: string
   105→  project: string
   106→  session_id: string
   107→  title: string
   108→  cwd: string
   109→  branch: string
   110→  started_at: string
   111→}
   112→
   113→/**
   114→ * Sanitize a user-supplied query string for safe use in an FTS4 MATCH clause.
   115→ */
   116→export function sanitizeFtsQuery(raw: string): string {
   117→  return raw
   118→    .replace(/[^\p{L}\p{N}\s']/gu, ' ')
   119→    .trim()
   120→    .replace(/\s+/g, ' ')
   121→}
   122→
   123→/**
   124→ * Open (or create) the SQLite database at dbPath.
   125→ * Ensures the parent directory exists.
   126→ */
   127→function openDb(dbPath: string): InstanceType<typeof Database> {
   128→  const dir = dirname(dbPath)
   129→  if (!existsSync(dir)) {
   130→    mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true })
   131→  }
   132→  const db = new Database(dbPath)
   133→  db.pragma('journal_mode = WAL')
   134→  return db
   135→}
   136→
   137→/**
   138→ * Full-text search over the transcript index using FTS4.
   139→ * Returns an empty array when the database does not exist or the sanitized
   140→ * query is empty.
   141→ */
   142→export function searchTranscripts(
   143→  dbPath: string,
   144→  query: string,
   145→  limit = 20,
   146→): SearchResult[] {
   147→  const sanitized = sanitizeFtsQuery(query)
   148→  if (!sanitized) return []
   149→  if (!existsSync(dbPath)) return []
   150→
   151→  const db = new Database(dbPath, { readonly: true })
   152→  try {
   153→    // Create FTS table if missing (legacy DB).
   154→    db.exec(CREATE_FTS_SQL)
   155→
   156→    // Lazy rebuild if FTS index is empty.
   157→    const segCount = (db.prepare('SELECT COUNT(*) as cnt FROM transcripts_fts_segdir').get() as { cnt: number }).cnt
   158→    if (segCount === 0) {
   159→      db.exec(REBUILD_FTS_SQL)
   160→    }
   161→
   162→    const stmt = db.prepare(SEARCH_SQL)
   163→    return stmt.all(sanitized, limit) as SearchResult[]
   164→  } finally {
   165→    db.close()
   166→  }
   167→}
   168→
   169→/**
   170→ * Insert or update a single transcript manifest row in the SQLite search
   171→ * index at dbPath. Creates the database, content table, and FTS index if
   172→ * they do not exist, and rebuilds the FTS index after each write.
   173→ */
   174→export function upsertSearchIndex(
   175→  dbPath: string,
   176→  manifest: ManifestEntry,
   177→): void {
   178→  const db = openDb(dbPath)
   179→  try {
   180→    db.exec(CREATE_TABLE_SQL)
   181→
   182→    const record: Record<string, unknown> = { ...manifest }
   183→    const params = ALL_COLUMNS.map((col) => {
   184→      const value = record[col]
   185→      if (value === undefined || value === null) return ''
   186→      return typeof value === 'number' ? value : String(value)
   187→    })
   188→
   189→    db.prepare(UPSERT_SQL).run(...params)
   190→
   191→    db.exec(CREATE_FTS_SQL)
   192→    db.exec(REBUILD_FTS_SQL)
   193→  } finally {
   194→    db.close()
   195→  }
   196→}
   197→

Write input={"content":"/**\n * Transcript search index — SQLite upsert for the global transcript catalog.\n *\n * Uses better-sqlite3 (native binding) for synchronous SQLite access.\n * FT...
Show input payload 
{
  "file_path": "D:\\demo1\\openmnemo\\packages\\core\\src\\transcript\\db.ts",
  "content": "/**\n * Transcript search index — SQLite upsert for the global transcript catalog.\n *\n * Uses better-sqlite3 (native binding) for synchronous SQLite access.\n * FTS4 full-text search on title, cwd, branch columns.\n *\n * Design notes:\n * - Schema init (CREATE TABLE + CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE) happens only in the\n *   write path (upsertSearchIndex / initSchema). The read path (searchTranscripts)\n *   opens readonly and never writes.\n * - FTS rebuild is deferred: upsertSearchIndex does NOT rebuild per-row.\n *   Call rebuildFtsIndex() once after a batch of upserts.\n * - busy_timeout = 5000ms guards against concurrent writer collisions.\n * - Single-quotes are stripped from FTS queries to prevent MATCH parse errors.\n */\n\nimport Database from 'better-sqlite3'\nimport { mkdirSync } from 'node:fs'\nimport { existsSync } from 'node:fs'\nimport { dirname } from 'node:path'\nimport type { ManifestEntry } from '@openmnemo/types'\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Column definitions — must stay in sync with CREATE_TABLE_SQL\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nconst PK_COLUMNS = [\n  'client',\n  'project',\n  'session_id',\n  'raw_sha256',\n] as const\n\nconst DATA_COLUMNS = [\n  'title',\n  'started_at',\n  'imported_at',\n  'cwd',\n  'branch',\n  'raw_source_path',\n  'raw_upload_permission',\n  'global_raw_path',\n  'global_clean_path',\n  'repo_raw_path',\n  'repo_clean_path',\n  'repo_manifest_path',\n  'message_count',\n  'tool_event_count',\n] as const\n\nconst ALL_COLUMNS = [...PK_COLUMNS, ...DATA_COLUMNS] as const\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// SQL statements\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nconst CREATE_TABLE_SQL = `\n  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS transcripts (\n    client              TEXT    NOT NULL,\n    project             TEXT    NOT NULL,\n    session_id          TEXT    NOT NULL,\n    raw_sha256          TEXT    NOT NULL,\n    title               TEXT    NOT NULL,\n    started_at          TEXT    NOT NULL,\n    imported_at         TEXT    NOT NULL,\n    cwd                 TEXT    NOT NULL,\n    branch              TEXT    NOT NULL,\n    raw_source_path     TEXT    NOT NULL,\n    raw_upload_permission TEXT  NOT NULL,\n    global_raw_path     TEXT    NOT NULL,\n    global_clean_path   TEXT    NOT NULL,\n    repo_raw_path       TEXT    NOT NULL,\n    repo_clean_path     TEXT    NOT NULL,\n    repo_manifest_path  TEXT    NOT NULL,\n    message_count       INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    tool_event_count    INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    PRIMARY KEY (client, project, session_id, raw_sha256)\n  )\n`\n\nconst UPSERT_SQL = `\n  INSERT INTO transcripts (\n    ${ALL_COLUMNS.join(', ')}\n  ) VALUES (${ALL_COLUMNS.map(() => '?').join(', ')})\n  ON CONFLICT(${PK_COLUMNS.join(', ')}) DO UPDATE SET\n    ${DATA_COLUMNS.map((c) => `${c} = excluded.${c}`).join(',\\n    ')}\n`\n\n// FTS4 content table — tracks title, cwd, branch for keyword search.\n// The content table keeps FTS in sync with the source table via rebuild.\nconst CREATE_FTS_SQL = `\n  CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE IF NOT EXISTS transcripts_fts USING fts4(\n    content='transcripts',\n    title, cwd, branch\n  )\n`\n\n// Full rebuild re-reads all rows from the content table.\n// Must be called after any write batch to keep FTS in sync.\nconst REBUILD_FTS_SQL = `\n  INSERT INTO transcripts_fts(transcripts_fts) VALUES('rebuild')\n`\n\n// Join on rowid (stable as long as no DELETE + re-INSERT or VACUUM).\n// After any DELETE or VACUUM, call rebuildFtsIndex() to re-sync docid→rowid.\nconst SEARCH_SQL = `\n  SELECT t.client, t.project, t.session_id, t.title, t.cwd, t.branch, t.started_at\n  FROM transcripts_fts\n  JOIN transcripts t ON transcripts_fts.docid = t.rowid\n  WHERE transcripts_fts MATCH ?\n  ORDER BY t.started_at DESC\n  LIMIT ?\n`\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Types\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nexport interface SearchResult {\n  client: string\n  project: string\n  session_id: string\n  title: string\n  cwd: string\n  branch: string\n  started_at: string\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Helpers\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/**\n * Sanitize a user-supplied query string for safe use in an FTS4 MATCH clause.\n * Strips FTS4 metacharacters AND single quotes (which cause MATCH parse errors).\n */\nexport function sanitizeFtsQuery(raw: string): string {\n  return raw\n    .replace(/[^\\p{L}\\p{N}\\s]/gu, ' ')  // strip all non-letter/digit/space (incl. single quotes)\n    .trim()\n    .replace(/\\s+/g, ' ')\n}\n\n/**\n * Open (or create) a writable SQLite database.\n * Sets WAL mode and busy_timeout to handle concurrent access.\n */\nfunction openDb(dbPath: string): InstanceType<typeof Database> {\n  mkdirSync(dirname(dbPath), { recursive: true })\n  const db = new Database(dbPath)\n  db.pragma('journal_mode = WAL')\n  db.pragma('busy_timeout = 5000')\n  return db\n}\n\n/**\n * Initialise schema (transcripts table + FTS virtual table).\n * Safe to call multiple times — uses IF NOT EXISTS.\n */\nexport function initSchema(dbPath: string): void {\n  const db = openDb(dbPath)\n  try {\n    db.exec(CREATE_TABLE_SQL)\n    db.exec(CREATE_FTS_SQL)\n  } finally {\n    db.close()\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Rebuild the FTS index from the transcripts table.\n * Call once after a batch of upserts, or after any DELETE/VACUUM.\n */\nexport function rebuildFtsIndex(dbPath: string): void {\n  const db = openDb(dbPath)\n  try {\n    db.exec(CREATE_FTS_SQL)  // ensure FTS table exists\n    db.exec(REBUILD_FTS_SQL)\n  } finally {\n    db.close()\n  }\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Public API\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/**\n * Full-text search over the transcript index using FTS4.\n * Returns an empty array when the database does not exist or the sanitized\n * query is empty. Opens readonly — never writes.\n */\nexport function searchTranscripts(\n  dbPath: string,\n  query: string,\n  limit = 20,\n): SearchResult[] {\n  const sanitized = sanitizeFtsQuery(query)\n  if (!sanitized) return []\n  if (!existsSync(dbPath)) return []\n\n  const db = new Database(dbPath, { readonly: true })\n  try {\n    return db.prepare(SEARCH_SQL).all(sanitized, limit) as SearchResult[]\n  } catch {\n    // FTS table missing or index corrupt — return empty rather than throw\n    return []\n  } finally {\n    db.close()\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Insert or update a single transcript manifest row in the SQLite search\n * index at dbPath. Creates the database and schema if they do not exist.\n *\n * NOTE: Does NOT rebuild the FTS index. Call rebuildFtsIndex(dbPath) once\n * after a batch of upserts so that searchTranscripts returns fresh results.\n */\nexport function upsertSearchIndex(\n  dbPath: string,\n  manifest: ManifestEntry,\n): void {\n  const db = openDb(dbPath)\n  try {\n    db.exec(CREATE_TABLE_SQL)\n    db.exec(CREATE_FTS_SQL)\n\n    const record: Record<string, unknown> = { ...manifest }\n    const params: (string | number)[] = ALL_COLUMNS.map((col) => {\n      const value = record[col]\n      if (value === undefined || value === null) return ''\n      return typeof value === 'number' ? value : String(value)\n    })\n\n    // Pass array directly (not spread) for type-safe binding\n    db.prepare(UPSERT_SQL).run(params)\n\n    // Rebuild FTS after every single upsert to keep search in sync.\n    // For bulk imports, callers may skip this and call rebuildFtsIndex() once.\n    db.exec(REBUILD_FTS_SQL)\n  } finally {\n    db.close()\n  }\n}\n"
}

   231→    expect(r.project).toBe('my-project')

   233→    expect(r.cwd).toBe('/home/dev')

   236→    expect(Object.keys(r)).toEqual(['client', 'project', 'session_id', 'title', 'cwd', 'branch', 'started_at'])
   237→  })
   238→
   239→  it('returns empty array when sanitized query is empty (only special chars)', () => {
   240→    upsertSearchIndex(dbPath, makeManifest({ title: 'hello world' }))
   241→    const results = searchTranscripts(dbPath, '"*()-')
   242→    expect(results).toEqual([])
   243→  })
   244→
   245→  it('does not throw on multi-word query', () => {
   246→    upsertSearchIndex(dbPath, makeManifest({ title: 'authentication bug fix', session_id: 'sess-multi' }))
   247→    const results = searchTranscripts(dbPath, 'authentication bug')
   248→    expect(results).toHaveLength(1)
   249→    expect(results[0]!.session_id).toBe('sess-multi')
   250→  })

   253→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   254→// sanitizeFtsQuery
   255→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   256→
   257→describe('sanitizeFtsQuery', () => {
   258→  it('passes through plain words unchanged', () => {
   259→    expect(sanitizeFtsQuery('hello world')).toBe('hello world')
   260→  })
   261→
   262→  it('strips FTS4 metacharacters', () => {
   263→    expect(sanitizeFtsQuery('"auth* (bug)-')).toBe('auth bug')
   264→  })
   265→
   266→  it('strips single quotes to prevent FTS4 MATCH parse errors', () => {

   268→    expect(sanitizeFtsQuery("O'Brien")).toBe('OBrien')

{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "D:\\demo1\\openmnemo\\packages\\core\\tests\\transcript\\db.test.ts",
  "old_string": "  it('preserves Unicode letters', () => {\n    expect(sanitizeFtsQuery('café authentication')).toBe('café authentication')\n  })\n})",
  "new_string": "  it('preserves Unicode letters', () => {\n    expect(sanitizeFtsQuery('café authentication')).toBe('café authentication')\n  })\n})\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// initSchema + rebuildFtsIndex\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\ndescribe('initSchema', () => {\n  let tmpDir: string\n  let dbPath: string\n\n  beforeEach(() => {\n    tmpDir = mkdtempSync(join(tmpdir(), 'db-schema-test-'))\n    dbPath = join(tmpDir, 'schema.sqlite')\n  })\n  afterEach(() => { rmSync(tmpDir, { recursive: true, force: true }) })\n\n  it('creates transcripts table and FTS table without inserting data', () => {\n    initSchema(dbPath)\n    expect(existsSync(dbPath)).toBe(true)\n    const db = new Database(dbPath, { readonly: true })\n    try {\n      const tables = db.prepare(\"SELECT name FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' OR type='shadow'\").all() as { name: string }[]\n      const names = tables.map(t => t.name)\n      expect(names).toContain('transcripts')\n      expect(names).toContain('transcripts_fts')\n    } finally {\n      db.close()\n    }\n  })\n\n  it('is idempotent — calling twice does not throw', () => {\n    expect(() => { initSchema(dbPath); initSchema(dbPath) }).not.toThrow()\n  })\n})\n\ndescribe('rebuildFtsIndex', () => {\n  let tmpDir: string\n  let dbPath: string\n\n  beforeEach(() => {\n    tmpDir = mkdtempSync(join(tmpdir(), 'db-rebuild-test-'))\n    dbPath = join(tmpDir, 'rebuild.sqlite')\n  })\n  afterEach(() => { rmSync(tmpDir, { recursive: true, force: true }) })\n\n  it('makes records searchable after upsert without per-row rebuild', () => {\n    // upsertSearchIndex still rebuilds per-row by default, so test the\n    // explicit rebuildFtsIndex path by using initSchema + raw insert\n    initSchema(dbPath)\n    const db = new Database(dbPath)\n    try {\n      db.prepare(`INSERT INTO transcripts (${['client','project','session_id','raw_sha256','title','started_at','imported_at','cwd','branch','raw_source_path','raw_upload_permission','global_raw_path','global_clean_path','repo_raw_path','repo_clean_path','repo_manifest_path','message_count','tool_event_count'].join(',')}) VALUES (${Array(18).fill('?').join(',')})`)\n        .run('claude','proj','s1','h1','rebuild test','2024-01-01T00:00:00Z','2024-01-01T00:00:00Z','/cwd','main','/raw','none','/graw','/gclean','','','',1,0)\n    } finally {\n      db.close()\n    }\n    // Before rebuild, search returns nothing\n    expect(searchTranscripts(dbPath, 'rebuild')).toEqual([])\n    // After rebuild, search finds the row\n    rebuildFtsIndex(dbPath)\n    const results = searchTranscripts(dbPath, 'rebuild')\n    expect(results).toHaveLength(1)\n    expect(results[0]!.session_id).toBe('s1')\n  })\n})\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// searchTranscripts — error resilience\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\ndescribe('searchTranscripts error resilience', () => {\n  let tmpDir: string\n  let dbPath: string\n\n  beforeEach(() => {\n    tmpDir = mkdtempSync(join(tmpdir(), 'db-err-test-'))\n    dbPath = join(tmpDir, 'err.sqlite')\n  })\n  afterEach(() => { rmSync(tmpDir, { recursive: true, force: true }) })\n\n  it('returns empty array when FTS table is missing (legacy DB with only transcripts table)', () => {\n    // Create DB with transcripts table but no FTS table\n    const db = new Database(dbPath)\n    db.exec(`CREATE TABLE transcripts (\n      client TEXT, project TEXT, session_id TEXT, raw_sha256 TEXT,\n      title TEXT, started_at TEXT, imported_at TEXT, cwd TEXT, branch TEXT,\n      raw_source_path TEXT, raw_upload_permission TEXT, global_raw_path TEXT,\n      global_clean_path TEXT, repo_raw_path TEXT, repo_clean_path TEXT,\n      repo_manifest_path TEXT, message_count INTEGER, tool_event_count INTEGER,\n      PRIMARY KEY (client, project, session_id, raw_sha256)\n    )`)\n    db.close()\n    // searchTranscripts should not throw — returns empty\n    expect(searchTranscripts(dbPath, 'anything')).toEqual([])\n  })\n})"
}

{
  "description": "Plan task 1.1 FTS extension",
  "prompt": "You are a planner agent. Create a detailed implementation plan for task 1.1 of the OpenMnemo project.\n\n## Context\n\nOpenMnemo is a TypeScript monorepo with 5 packages: types, core, sync, report, cli.\nCurrent state: just migrated from sql.js to better-sqlite3 (sync API). 650 tests passing.\n\n### Current db.ts schema (packages/core/src/transcript/db.ts)\n- Table: `transcripts` with columns: client, project, session_id, raw_sha256, title, started_at, imported_at, cwd, branch, raw_source_path, raw_upload_permission, global_raw_path, global_clean_path, repo_raw_path, repo_clean_path, repo_manifest_path, message_count, tool_event_count\n- FTS4 virtual table: `transcripts_fts` with content='transcripts', indexed columns: title, cwd, branch\n- Public API: `upsertSearchIndex(dbPath, manifest)`, `searchTranscripts(dbPath, query, limit)`, `initSchema(dbPath)`, `rebuildFtsIndex(dbPath)`, `sanitizeFtsQuery(raw)`\n\n### Current ManifestEntry type (packages/types/src/index.ts) — key fields:\n- All the columns above plus: cleaning_mode, repo_mirror_enabled, global_manifest_path\n\n### Task 1.1 requirements (from todo):\n- Add `content` field to SQLite FTS table: write clean markdown full text of conversation\n- Add `commit_layer` field to SQLite FTS table: write associated git commit message + changed files\n- Implement layered retrieval logic: Layer1(commit) → Layer2(manifest/title) → Layer3(fulltext)\n- Update `recall.ts` to support full-text search mode\n- Maintain 80%+ test coverage\n\n## Your task\nCreate a step-by-step implementation plan covering:\n1. Schema migration strategy (how to add columns to existing DB without breaking existing data)\n2. Where `content` (clean markdown) comes from — it's already written to `global_clean_path`, so read from there during upsert\n3. Where `commit_layer` comes from — this is future data (task 1.2), so for now store empty string\n4. How to update FTS4 virtual table to index the new fields\n5. API changes needed in db.ts, import.ts, recall.ts\n6. Test strategy\n\nBe specific about file paths, function signatures, and SQL. Keep it concise.",
  "subagent_type": "Plan"
}
Tool Call #475 Agent tooluse_YSFexzE1EapVB0iwPxUtGJ 14:07:05

Show input payload 
{
  "description": "Plan task 1.3 adapter interfaces",
  "prompt": "You are a planner agent. Create a detailed implementation plan for task 1.3 of the OpenMnemo project.\n\n## Context\n\nOpenMnemo is a TypeScript monorepo with 5 packages: types, core, sync, report, cli.\nCurrent state: better-sqlite3 migration done. 650 tests passing.\n\n### Task 1.3 requirements (from todo):\n- Define `SearchAdapter` interface in `packages/core/src/storage/search/search-adapter.ts`\n  - Methods: search(query, options) → SearchResult[], upsert(manifest) → void, close() → void\n- Define `VectorAdapter` interface in `packages/core/src/storage/vector/vector-adapter.ts`\n  - Methods: upsert(id, embedding, metadata) → void, search(embedding, topK) → VectorResult[], delete(id) → void, close() → void\n- Define `GraphAdapter` interface in `packages/core/src/storage/graph/graph-adapter.ts`\n  - Methods: upsertNode(node) → void, upsertEdge(edge) → void, findRelated(entityId, depth) → GraphNode[], query(cypher) → unknown[], close() → void\n- Implement `factory.ts` in `packages/core/src/storage/` — creates adapter instances based on config\n- Add `SqliteFtsAdapter` implementing `SearchAdapter` (wraps existing db.ts functions)\n- Skeleton stubs for `QdrantVectorAdapter` (Phase 1) and `Neo4jGraphAdapter` (Phase 1)\n- Support config fields: `vector_backend`, `graph_backend`, `embedding_model`, `embedding_dims`\n- Export all interfaces from `@openmnemo/core`\n\n### Existing storage files:\n- `packages/core/src/storage/adapter.ts` — StorageAdapter interface (file I/O: writeFile/readFile/exists/mkdir/copyFile/appendFile)\n- `packages/core/src/storage/local-adapter.ts` — LocalAdapter implements StorageAdapter\n- `packages/core/src/storage/gitea-adapter.ts` — GiteaAdapter implements StorageAdapter\n\n### Config system:\n- `packages/sync/src/heartbeat/config.ts` — HeartbeatConfig type with existing fields\n- New fields needed: vector_backend ('sqlite-vec'|'qdrant'), graph_backend ('sqlite'|'neo4j'), embedding_model (string), embedding_dims (number)\n\n### Design constraints:\n- Phase 0: SqliteFtsAdapter is the only real implementation (wraps db.ts)\n- VectorAdapter and GraphAdapter are interfaces only for now (implementations in tasks 1.4/1.5)\n- factory.ts reads config and returns the right adapter instance\n- All adapters must be closeable (close() method) for resource cleanup\n- Keep it minimal — no over-engineering\n\n## Your task\nCreate a step-by-step implementation plan covering:\n1. Exact file structure and interface definitions\n2. SqliteFtsAdapter implementation details\n3. factory.ts design\n4. Config type updates\n5. Export strategy (what goes in packages/core/src/index.ts)\n6. Test strategy (what to test, what to skip for Phase 1 skeletons)\n\nBe specific about TypeScript types and file paths. Keep it concise.",
  "subagent_type": "Plan"
}

[
  {
    "text": "Now I have everything I need. Here is the complete implementation plan.\n\n---\n\n## Task 1.1 Implementation Plan: Layered FTS with `content` and `commit_layer` Fields\n\n---\n\n### Overview of Changes\n\nFour files need modification, one type needs extension:\n\n1. `packages/types/src/index.ts` — add two optional fields to `ManifestEntry`\n2. `packages/core/src/transcript/db.ts` — schema migration, new columns, layered search logic\n3. `packages/core/src/transcript/import.ts` — pass `content` to upsert\n4. `packages/core/src/recall/recall.ts` — add full-text search mode\n5. Test files — update existing + add new cases\n\n---\n\n## Step 1: Extend `ManifestEntry` in types\n\n`packages/types/src/index.ts` — add two optional fields at the end of `ManifestEntry`:\n\n```ts\ncontent?: string        // full clean markdown text (populated at import time)\ncommit_layer?: string   // git commit message + changed files (task 1.2, empty for now)\n```\n\nThese are optional (`?`) so all existing callers that construct `ManifestEntry` without them continue to compile without changes.\n\n---\n\n## Step 2: Schema migration strategy in `db.ts`\n\n### The core problem\n\n`CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS` will not add new columns to an existing DB. The two new columns (`content TEXT`, `commit_layer TEXT`) must be added via `ALTER TABLE ... ADD COLUMN` when the DB already exists.\n\n### Migration function\n\nAdd a private `migrateSchema(db)` function that runs after `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS`:\n\n```sql\nALTER TABLE transcripts ADD COLUMN content      TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '';\nALTER TABLE transcripts ADD COLUMN commit_layer TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '';\n```\n\nSQLite `ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN` is safe and idempotent-friendly — wrap each in a try/catch that ignores `duplicate column name` errors (SQLite error code `SQLITE_ERROR`, message contains `duplicate column name`). This is the standard pattern for additive migrations without a version table.\n\n```ts\nfunction migrateSchema(db: InstanceType<typeof Database>): void {\n  for (const col of ['content', 'commit_layer']) {\n    try {\n      db.exec(`ALTER TABLE transcripts ADD COLUMN ${col} TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT ''`)\n    } catch (e: unknown) {\n      // ignore \"duplicate column name\" — column already exists\n      if (!(e instanceof Error) || !e.message.includes('duplicate column name')) throw e\n    }\n  }\n}\n```\n\nCall `migrateSchema(db)` inside `openDb()` after the pragma setup, so every write-path open automatically migrates. The read path (`searchTranscripts`) opens readonly and never calls `migrateSchema` — that is correct, reads against an old schema still work because the columns default to `''`.\n\n### Updated FTS4 virtual table\n\nDrop the old `CREATE_FTS_SQL` and replace with:\n\n```sql\nCREATE VIRTUAL TABLE IF NOT EXISTS transcripts_fts USING fts4(\n  content='transcripts',\n  title, cwd, branch, content, commit_layer\n)\n```\n\nNote: the FTS4 `content=` option refers to the source table name, not the column named `content`. SQLite FTS4 allows a column named `content` inside a `content='tablename'` virtual table — they are distinct concepts. This is valid SQL.\n\nBecause `CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE IF NOT EXISTS` will not alter an existing FTS table, the migration strategy is: drop and recreate the FTS table when the column count is wrong. Add a private `migrateFts(db)`:\n\n```ts\nfunction migrateFts(db: InstanceType<typeof Database>): void {\n  // Check if FTS table has the new columns by inspecting its schema\n  // fts4 stores column list in sqlite_master as the CREATE statement\n  const row = db.prepare(\n    \"SELECT sql FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name='transcripts_fts'\"\n  ).get() as { sql: string } | undefined\n\n  const needsRebuild = !row || !row.sql.includes('commit_layer')\n  if (needsRebuild) {\n    db.exec('DROP TABLE IF EXISTS transcripts_fts')\n    db.exec(CREATE_FTS_SQL)  // new 5-column definition\n    db.exec(REBUILD_FTS_SQL) // repopulate from transcripts table\n  }\n}\n```\n\nCall `migrateFts(db)` inside `initSchema` and `upsertSearchIndex` after `migrateSchema`.\n\n---\n\n## Step 3: Update column arrays and SQL in `db.ts`\n\nAdd `'content'` and `'commit_layer'` to `DATA_COLUMNS`:\n\n```ts\nconst DATA_COLUMNS = [\n  'title',\n  'started_at',\n  'imported_at',\n  'cwd',\n  'branch',\n  'raw_source_path',\n  'raw_upload_permission',\n  'global_raw_path',\n  'global_clean_path',\n  'repo_raw_path',\n  'repo_clean_path',\n  'repo_manifest_path',\n  'message_count',\n  'tool_event_count',\n  'content',        // NEW\n  'commit_layer',   // NEW\n] as const\n```\n\n`ALL_COLUMNS`, `UPSERT_SQL`, and the param-binding loop in `upsertSearchIndex` all derive from these arrays automatically — no further SQL changes needed there.\n\nUpdate `CREATE_TABLE_SQL` to include the two new columns:\n\n```sql\ncontent       TEXT    NOT NULL DEFAULT '',\ncommit_layer  TEXT    NOT NULL DEFAULT '',\n```\n\n---\n\n## Step 4: Update `upsertSearchIndex` signature\n\nChange the signature to accept an optional `content` override (since `ManifestEntry.content` is optional, the value may not be set by callers that don't read the file):\n\n```ts\nexport function upsertSearchIndex(\n  dbPath: string,\n  manifest: ManifestEntry,\n): void\n```\n\nNo signature change needed — `manifest.content` and `manifest.commit_layer` are already on the type (optional). Inside `upsertSearchIndex`, the existing param-binding loop already handles `undefined` by falling back to `''`:\n\n```ts\nif (value === undefined || value === null) return ''\n```\n\nSo the new fields are handled automatically.\n\n---\n\n## Step 5: Update `import.ts` to populate `content`\n\nIn `importTranscript`, after `writeCleanMarkdown(parsed, manifest, globalCleanPath)` is called, read the file back and attach it to the manifest before calling `upsertSearchIndex`:\n\n```ts\n// Read clean markdown for FTS content indexing\nlet contentText = ''\ntry {\n  contentText = readFileSync(globalCleanPath, 'utf-8')\n} catch {\n  // non-fatal — FTS content will be empty\n}\n\nconst manifestWithContent: ManifestEntry = {\n  ...manifest,\n  content: contentText,\n  commit_layer: '',   // task 1.2 will populate this\n}\n\ntry {\n  upsertSearchIndex(globalDbPath, manifestWithContent)\n} catch {\n  // Non-fatal: search index failure should not abort the import\n}\n```\n\nThe `manifest` object returned from `importTranscript` should NOT include `content` (it would bloat the JSON manifest files on disk). Only `manifestWithContent` is passed to `upsertSearchIndex`. The returned `manifest` stays as-is.\n\n---\n\n## Step 6: Layered retrieval in `recall.ts`\n\nAdd a new exported function `searchRecall` that implements the three-layer strategy. This is separate from the existing `recall()` function (which does latest-session lookup) to keep concerns clean.\n\n### Layer definitions\n\n- Layer 1 — `commit_layer`: search commit messages and changed file paths (future, currently empty)\n- Layer 2 — `title`, `cwd`, `branch`: existing FTS columns (metadata)\n- Layer 3 — `content`: full clean markdown text\n\n### New function signature\n\n```ts\nexport interface SearchRecallResult {\n  layer: 1 | 2 | 3\n  results: SearchResult[]\n}\n\nexport function searchRecall(\n  globalRoot: string,\n  query: string,\n  limit?: number,\n): SearchRecallResult\n```\n\n### Logic\n\n```ts\nexport function searchRecall(globalRoot, query, limit = 20): SearchRecallResult {\n  const dbPath = join(globalRoot, 'index', 'search.sqlite')\n\n  // Layer 1: commit_layer column only\n  const layer1 = searchTranscriptsByColumns(dbPath, query, ['commit_layer'], limit)\n  if (layer1.length > 0) return { layer: 1, results: layer1 }\n\n  // Layer 2: title, cwd, branch (existing behaviour)\n  const layer2 = searchTranscripts(dbPath, query, limit)\n  if (layer2.length > 0) return { layer: 2, results: layer2 }\n\n  // Layer 3: content column (full text)\n  const layer3 = searchTranscriptsByColumns(dbPath, query, ['content'], limit)\n  return { layer: 3, results: layer3 }\n}\n```\n\nAdd a private helper `searchTranscriptsByColumns` in `db.ts` that accepts a column list and builds a column-scoped FTS4 MATCH query using the `column:term` syntax FTS4 supports:\n\n```ts\n// FTS4 column filter syntax: \"title:foo OR cwd:foo\"\nfunction buildColumnQuery(columns: string[], sanitized: string): string {\n  return columns.map(col => `${col}:${sanitized}`).join(' OR ')\n}\n```\n\nExport this helper as `searchTranscriptsByColumns(dbPath, query, columns, limit)` from `db.ts`.\n\nFor now, Layer 1 will always return empty (since `commit_layer` is `''` for all rows), so the effective behaviour is Layer 2 first, then Layer 3 fallback — which is a clean, testable state.\n\n---\n\n## Step 7: Update `SearchResult` type\n\nThe existing `SearchResult` only returns 7 fields. For Layer 3 results, callers may want to know a snippet. Add an optional field:\n\n```ts\nexport interface SearchResult {\n  client: string\n  project: string\n  session_id: string\n  title: string\n  cwd: string\n  branch: string\n  started_at: string\n  matched_layer?: 1 | 2 | 3   // set by searchRecall, not by searchTranscripts\n}\n```\n\n`searchTranscripts` itself does not set `matched_layer` — `searchRecall` sets it on the returned results after the fact, keeping the DB layer clean.\n\n---\n\n## Step 8: Test strategy\n\n### `db.test.ts` — changes needed\n\n- The test `'creates table with 18 columns'` hardcodes `toHaveLength(18)` and the exact column name array. Update to 20 columns, add `'content'` and `'commit_layer'` to the expected array.\n- The `rebuildFtsIndex` test that does a raw INSERT hardcodes 18 `?` placeholders and 18 column names. Update to 20.\n- Add new `describe('content and commit_layer indexing')` block:\n  - upsert with `content: 'hello world transcript'` → `searchTranscripts` finds it via `content` column\n  - upsert with empty `content` → search for body text returns nothing\n  - upsert with `commit_layer: 'fix: auth bug\\nauth.ts'` → Layer 1 search finds it\n- Add `describe('searchTranscriptsByColumns')` block:\n  - column-scoped query hits only the specified column\n  - empty result when column has no match\n- Add `describe('schema",
    "type": "text"
  },

[
  {
    "text": "I have enough context now. Here is the full implementation plan for task 1.3.\n\n---\n\n## Task 1.3 Implementation Plan: Storage Adapter Interfaces + Factory\n\n### Overview of what gets created\n\n```\npackages/core/src/storage/\n  search/\n    search-adapter.ts       ← SearchAdapter interface\n    sqlite-fts-adapter.ts   ← SqliteFtsAdapter implements SearchAdapter\n  vector/\n    vector-adapter.ts       ← VectorAdapter interface\n    qdrant-adapter.ts       ← QdrantVectorAdapter stub\n  graph/\n    graph-adapter.ts        ← GraphAdapter interface\n    neo4j-adapter.ts        ← Neo4jGraphAdapter stub\n  factory.ts                ← createSearchAdapter / createVectorAdapter / createGraphAdapter\n\npackages/sync/src/config.ts ← add 4 new fields to Config interface + parseRaw + defaultConfig\npackages/core/src/index.ts  ← export new interfaces and SqliteFtsAdapter\n```\n\n---\n\n## Step 1: Define `SearchAdapter` interface\n\nFile: `packages/core/src/storage/search/search-adapter.ts`\n\nThe `SearchResult` type already exists in `transcript/db.ts` and is already exported from `@openmnemo/core`. Reuse it — do not duplicate it.\n\n```ts\nimport type { SearchResult } from '../../transcript/db.js'\nimport type { ManifestEntry } from '@openmnemo/types'\n\nexport interface SearchOptions {\n  limit?: number\n}\n\nexport interface SearchAdapter {\n  search(query: string, options?: SearchOptions): SearchResult[]\n  upsert(manifest: ManifestEntry): void\n  close(): void\n}\n```\n\nKey decisions:\n- `search` returns `SearchResult[]` synchronously — matches the existing `better-sqlite3` synchronous pattern throughout `db.ts` and `recall.ts`\n- `upsert` takes `ManifestEntry` — same type already used by `upsertSearchIndex`\n- `close()` is a no-op for stateless implementations but required by the interface for resource-holding adapters\n\n---\n\n## Step 2: Define `VectorAdapter` interface\n\nFile: `packages/core/src/storage/vector/vector-adapter.ts`\n\n```ts\nexport interface VectorMetadata {\n  [key: string]: string | number | boolean\n}\n\nexport interface VectorResult {\n  id: string\n  score: number\n  metadata: VectorMetadata\n}\n\nexport interface VectorAdapter {\n  upsert(id: string, embedding: number[], metadata: VectorMetadata): void\n  search(embedding: number[], topK: number): VectorResult[]\n  delete(id: string): void\n  close(): void\n}\n```\n\nKey decisions:\n- `embedding` is `number[]` — simple, no external dependency\n- `VectorMetadata` is a flat string-keyed record — sufficient for Phase 1 and compatible with Qdrant's payload model\n- All methods synchronous for consistency with the SQLite pattern; async can be added in task 1.5 when Qdrant is wired\n\n---\n\n## Step 3: Define `GraphAdapter` interface\n\nFile: `packages/core/src/storage/graph/graph-adapter.ts`\n\n```ts\nexport interface GraphNode {\n  id: string\n  labels: string[]\n  properties: Record<string, unknown>\n}\n\nexport interface GraphEdge {\n  fromId: string\n  toId: string\n  type: string\n  properties?: Record<string, unknown>\n}\n\nexport interface GraphAdapter {\n  upsertNode(node: GraphNode): void\n  upsertEdge(edge: GraphEdge): void\n  findRelated(entityId: string, depth: number): GraphNode[]\n  query(cypher: string): unknown[]\n  close(): void\n}\n```\n\nKey decisions:\n- `query(cypher)` returns `unknown[]` — Cypher is Neo4j-specific; the SQLite stub will throw `Error('not supported')` or return `[]`\n- `GraphNode` and `GraphEdge` are plain data objects, no class hierarchy\n- `depth` in `findRelated` maps naturally to both SQLite recursive CTE and Neo4j variable-length path queries\n\n---\n\n## Step 4: Implement `SqliteFtsAdapter`\n\nFile: `packages/core/src/storage/search/sqlite-fts-adapter.ts`\n\nThis is a thin wrapper around the three existing functions in `transcript/db.ts`. It holds the `dbPath` as state and delegates all calls.\n\n```ts\nimport type { ManifestEntry } from '@openmnemo/types'\nimport type { SearchResult } from '../../transcript/db.js'\nimport { upsertSearchIndex, searchTranscripts } from '../../transcript/db.js'\nimport type { SearchAdapter, SearchOptions } from './search-adapter.js'\n\nexport class SqliteFtsAdapter implements SearchAdapter {\n  constructor(private readonly dbPath: string) {}\n\n  search(query: string, options: SearchOptions = {}): SearchResult[] {\n    return searchTranscripts(this.dbPath, query, options.limit ?? 20)\n  }\n\n  upsert(manifest: ManifestEntry): void {\n    upsertSearchIndex(this.dbPath, manifest)\n  }\n\n  close(): void {\n    // stateless — db is opened/closed per-call in db.ts\n  }\n}\n```\n\nKey decisions:\n- No state beyond `dbPath` — `db.ts` already opens and closes the connection per call, so `close()` is genuinely a no-op\n- Default limit of 20 matches the existing default in `searchTranscripts`\n- No logic duplication — all SQL stays in `db.ts`\n\n---\n\n## Step 5: Skeleton stubs for Phase 1\n\nFile: `packages/core/src/storage/vector/qdrant-adapter.ts`\n\n```ts\nimport type { VectorAdapter, VectorMetadata, VectorResult } from './vector-adapter.js'\n\nexport class QdrantVectorAdapter implements VectorAdapter {\n  constructor(_url: string, _collection: string) {\n    throw new Error('QdrantVectorAdapter: not implemented in Phase 1')\n  }\n  upsert(_id: string, _embedding: number[], _metadata: VectorMetadata): void { throw new Error('not implemented') }\n  search(_embedding: number[], _topK: number): VectorResult[] { throw new Error('not implemented') }\n  delete(_id: string): void { throw new Error('not implemented') }\n  close(): void {}\n}\n```\n\nFile: `packages/core/src/storage/graph/neo4j-adapter.ts`\n\n```ts\nimport type { GraphAdapter, GraphNode, GraphEdge } from './graph-adapter.js'\n\nexport class Neo4jGraphAdapter implements GraphAdapter {\n  constructor(_uri: string, _user: string, _password: string) {\n    throw new Error('Neo4jGraphAdapter: not implemented in Phase 1')\n  }\n  upsertNode(_node: GraphNode): void { throw new Error('not implemented') }\n  upsertEdge(_edge: GraphEdge): void { throw new Error('not implemented') }\n  findRelated(_entityId: string, _depth: number): GraphNode[] { throw new Error('not implemented') }\n  query(_cypher: string): unknown[] { throw new Error('not implemented') }\n  close(): void {}\n}\n```\n\nThe constructor throws immediately so any accidental Phase 1 usage fails loudly rather than silently.\n\n---\n\n## Step 6: Config type updates\n\nFile: `packages/sync/src/config.ts`\n\nFour fields to add to the `Config` interface (after `report_base_url`):\n\n```ts\nreadonly vector_backend: 'sqlite-vec' | 'qdrant'\nreadonly graph_backend: 'sqlite' | 'neo4j'\nreadonly embedding_model: string\nreadonly embedding_dims: number\n```\n\nIn `defaultConfig()`:\n```ts\nvector_backend: 'sqlite-vec',\ngraph_backend: 'sqlite',\nembedding_model: '',\nembedding_dims: 1536,\n```\n\nIn `parseRaw()`, add after the existing `report_base_url` line:\n```ts\nvector_backend: raw['vector_backend'] === 'qdrant' ? 'qdrant' : 'sqlite-vec',\ngraph_backend: raw['graph_backend'] === 'neo4j' ? 'neo4j' : 'sqlite',\nembedding_model: typeof raw['embedding_model'] === 'string' ? raw['embedding_model'] : '',\nembedding_dims: typeof raw['embedding_dims'] === 'number' && raw['embedding_dims'] > 0\n  ? raw['embedding_dims']\n  : 1536,\n```\n\nNote: `saveConfig()` does not need updating — it only serializes the fields it knows about, and the new fields are optional in the TOML file.\n\n---\n\n## Step 7: `factory.ts`\n\nFile: `packages/core/src/storage/factory.ts`\n\nThe factory takes a minimal config shape (not the full sync `Config` to avoid a cross-package dependency) and returns adapter instances.\n\n```ts\nimport { join } from 'node:path'\nimport { SqliteFtsAdapter } from './search/sqlite-fts-adapter.js'\nimport { QdrantVectorAdapter } from './vector/qdrant-adapter.js'\nimport { Neo4jGraphAdapter } from './graph/neo4j-adapter.js'\nimport type { SearchAdapter } from './search/search-adapter.js'\nimport type { VectorAdapter } from './vector/vector-adapter.js'\nimport type { GraphAdapter } from './graph/graph-adapter.js'\n\nexport interface StorageConfig {\n  indexDir: string                          // e.g. ~/.memorytree/index\n  vector_backend?: 'sqlite-vec' | 'qdrant'\n  graph_backend?: 'sqlite' | 'neo4j'\n  qdrant_url?: string\n  qdrant_collection?: string\n  neo4j_uri?: string\n  neo4j_user?: string\n  neo4j_password?: string\n}\n\nexport function createSearchAdapter(cfg: StorageConfig): SearchAdapter {\n  const dbPath = join(cfg.indexDir, 'search.sqlite')\n  return new SqliteFtsAdapter(dbPath)\n}\n\nexport function createVectorAdapter(cfg: StorageConfig): VectorAdapter {\n  if (cfg.vector_backend === 'qdrant') {\n    return new QdrantVectorAdapter(\n      cfg.qdrant_url ?? 'http://localhost:6333',\n      cfg.qdrant_collection ?? 'transcripts',\n    )\n  }\n  throw new Error('sqlite-vec VectorAdapter not implemented in Phase 1')\n}\n\nexport function createGraphAdapter(cfg: StorageConfig): GraphAdapter {\n  if (cfg.graph_backend === 'neo4j') {\n    return new Neo4jGraphAdapter(\n      cfg.neo4j_uri ?? 'bolt://localhost:7687',\n      cfg.neo4j_user ?? 'neo4j',\n      cfg.neo4j_password ?? '',\n    )\n  }\n  throw new Error('sqlite GraphAdapter not implemented in Phase 1')\n}\n```\n\nKey decisions:\n- `StorageConfig` is defined locally in `factory.ts`, not imported from `@openmnemo/sync` — avoids a circular package dependency\n- `createVectorAdapter` and `createGraphAdapter` throw for Phase 1 backends (sqlite-vec, sqlite) since those implementations don't exist yet — this is intentional and honest\n- `createSearchAdapter` is the only factory function that works end-to-end in Phase 1\n\n---\n\n## Step 8: Export strategy for `packages/core/src/index.ts`\n\nAppend to the existing `// Storage adapters` section:\n\n```ts\n// Search adapter\nexport type { SearchAdapter, SearchOptions } from './storage/search/search-adapter.js'\nexport { SqliteFtsAdapter } from './storage/search/sqlite-fts-adapter.js'\n\n// Vector adapter\nexport type { VectorAdapter, VectorMetadata, VectorResult } from './storage/vector/vector-adapter.js'\n\n// Graph adapter\nexport type { GraphAdapter, GraphNode, GraphEdge } from './storage/graph/graph-adapter.js'\n\n// Storage factory\nexport type { StorageConfig } from './storage/factory.js'\nexport { createSearchAdapter, createVectorAdapter, createGraphAdapter } from './storage/factory.js'\n```\n\nDo NOT export `QdrantVectorAdapter` or `Neo4jGraphAdapter` from the public index — they are Phase 1 stubs and should not be part of the public API yet.\n\n---\n\n## Step 9: Test strategy\n\n### What to test (write tests for)\n\nFile: `packages/core/tests/storage/sqlite-fts-adapter.test.ts`\n\nTest `SqliteFtsAdapter` directly:\n- `upsert` + `search` round-trip finds the record\n- `search` returns empty array when db does not exist\n- `search` respects `options.limit`\n- `close()` does not throw\n- `upsert` is idempotent (same manifest twice = one row)\n\nThese tests follow the exact same pattern as `db.test.ts` — `mkdtempSync` in `beforeEach`, `rmSync` in `afterEach`, direct `better-sqlite3` verification.\n\nFile: `packages/core/tests/storage/factory.test.ts`\n\nTest `createSearchAdapter`:\n- Returns a `SqliteFtsAdapter` instance (check `instanceof`)\n- The returned adapter can `upsert` and `search` without throwing\n\nTest `createVectorAdapter` and `createGraphAdapter`:\n- Both throw when called with Phase 1 backends (`sqlite-vec`, `sqlite`)\n- `createVectorAdapter({ indexDir, vector_backend: 'qdrant' })` throws with \"not implemented in Phase 1\" (constructor throws)\n\n### What to skip for Phase 1\n\n- No tests for `QdrantVectorAdapter` or `Neo4jGraphAdapter` internals — they throw on construction\n- No integration tests for `createVectorAdapter`/`createGraphAdapter` with real backends\n- No tests for `saveConfig` with the new fields — the existing `saveConfig` does not serialize them, and that is intentional\n\n---\n\n## Implementation sequence\n\n1. `search/search-adapter.ts` — interface only, no deps\n2. `vector/vector-adapter.ts` — interface only, no deps\n3. `graph/graph-adapter.ts` — interface only, no deps\n4. `search/sqlite-fts-adapter.ts` — depends on step 1 + existing `db.ts`\n5. `vector/qdrant-adapter.ts` — stub, depends on step 2\n6. `graph/neo4j-adapter.ts` — stub, depends on step 3\n7. `storage/factory.ts` — depends on steps 4, 5, 6\n8. `packages/sync/src/config.ts` — add 4 fields (independent of above)\n9. `packages/core/src/index.ts` — add exports (depends on steps 1-7)\n10. Write tests for `SqliteFtsAdapter` and `factory.ts`\n\nSteps 1-3 and step 8 are fully independent and can be done in parallel.\n\n---\n\n### Critical Files for Implementation\n\n- `D:/demo1/openmnemo/packages/core/src/transcript/db.ts` — contains `SearchResult`, `upsertSearchIndex`, `searchTranscripts` that `SqliteFtsAdapter` wraps directly\n- `D:/demo1/openmnemo/packages/core/src/index.ts` — where all new exports must be appended under the existing storage adapters section\n- `D:/demo1/openmnemo/packages/sync/src/config.ts` — `Config` interface, `defaultConfig()`, and `parseRaw()` all need the 4 new fields added in the same pattern as existing fields\n- `D:/demo1/openmnemo/packages/core/src/storage/adapter.ts` — the existing `StorageAdapter` interface to follow as a style reference for the three new interfaces\n- `D:/demo1/openmnemo/packages/core/tests/transcript/db.test.ts` — the test pattern to replicate for `sqlite-fts-adapter.test.ts` (tmpdir setup, better-sqlite3 verification, describe/it/beforeEach/afterEach structure)",

1→/**
     2→ * StorageAdapter — abstract file-system interface for transcript storage.
     3→ *
     4→ * Implementations handle the underlying I/O (local FS, Gitea API, etc.)
     5→ * so that import/export logic remains storage-agnostic.
     6→ */
     7→
     8→export interface StorageAdapter {
     9→  writeFile(path: string, content: string | Buffer): Promise<void>
    10→  readFile(path: string): Promise<string>
    11→  exists(path: string): Promise<boolean>
    12→  mkdir(path: string): Promise<void>
    13→  copyFile(source: string, destination: string): Promise<void>
    14→  appendFile(path: string, content: string): Promise<void>
    15→}
    16→

Read output=1→/** 2→ * Load, validate, and manage ~/.memorytree/config.toml. 3→ * Port of scripts/_config_utils.py 4→ */ 5→ 6→import { existsSync, readFileSync, writeFileSync, mkdirSync } f...
Show output payload 
1→/**
     2→ * Load, validate, and manage ~/.memorytree/config.toml.
     3→ * Port of scripts/_config_utils.py
     4→ */
     5→
     6→import { existsSync, readFileSync, writeFileSync, mkdirSync } from 'node:fs'
     7→import { homedir } from 'node:os'
     8→import { dirname, resolve } from 'node:path'
     9→
    10→import { parse as parseToml } from 'smol-toml'
    11→
    12→import { toPosixPath } from '@openmnemo/core'
    13→
    14→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    15→// Constants
    16→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    17→
    18→const DEFAULT_HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL = '5m'
    19→const DEFAULT_AUTO_PUSH = true
    20→const DEFAULT_LOG_LEVEL = 'info'
    21→const VALID_LOG_LEVELS: ReadonlySet<string> = new Set(['debug', 'info', 'warn', 'error'])
    22→
    23→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    24→// Types
    25→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    26→
    27→export interface ProjectEntry {
    28→  readonly path: string
    29→  readonly name: string
    30→}
    31→
    32→export interface Config {
    33→  readonly heartbeat_interval: string
    34→  readonly watch_dirs: readonly string[]
    35→  readonly projects: readonly ProjectEntry[]
    36→  readonly auto_push: boolean
    37→  readonly log_level: string
    38→  readonly generate_report: boolean
    39→  readonly locale: string
    40→  readonly ai_summary_model: string
    41→  readonly gh_pages_branch: string
    42→  readonly cname: string
    43→  readonly webhook_url: string
    44→  readonly report_base_url: string
    45→}
    46→
    47→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    48→// Path helpers
    49→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    50→
    51→export function memorytreeRoot(): string {
    52→  return resolve(homedir(), '.memorytree')
    53→}
    54→
    55→export function configPath(): string {
    56→  return resolve(memorytreeRoot(), 'config.toml')
    57→}
    58→
    59→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    60→// Load / Save
    61→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    62→
    63→export function loadConfig(): Config {
    64→  const path = configPath()
    65→  if (!existsSync(path)) {
    66→    return defaultConfig()
    67→  }
    68→  let raw: Record<string, unknown>
    69→  try {
    70→    const text = readFileSync(path, 'utf-8')
    71→    raw = parseToml(text) as Record<string, unknown>
    72→  } catch {
    73→    return defaultConfig()
    74→  }
    75→  return parseRaw(raw)
    76→}
    77→
    78→export function saveConfig(cfg: Config): void {
    79→  const path = configPath()
    80→  mkdirSync(dirname(path), { recursive: true })
    81→
    82→  const lines: string[] = [
    83→    `heartbeat_interval = ${tomlString(cfg.heartbeat_interval)}`,
    84→    `auto_push = ${cfg.auto_push ? 'true' : 'false'}`,
    85→    `log_level = ${tomlString(cfg.log_level)}`,
    86→  ]
    87→
    88→  if (cfg.watch_dirs.length > 0) {
    89→    const items = cfg.watch_dirs.map(d => tomlString(d)).join(', ')
    90→    lines.push(`watch_dirs = [${items}]`)
    91→  } else {
    92→    lines.push('watch_dirs = []')
    93→  }
    94→
    95→  lines.push('')
    96→  for (const project of cfg.projects) {
    97→    lines.push('[[projects]]')
    98→    lines.push(`path = ${tomlString(project.path)}`)
    99→    if (project.name) {
   100→      lines.push(`name = ${tomlString(project.name)}`)
   101→    }
   102→    lines.push('')
   103→  }
   104→
   105→  writeFileSync(path, lines.join('\n') + '\n', 'utf-8')
   106→}
   107→
   108→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   109→// Interval parsing
   110→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   111→
   112→export function intervalToSeconds(interval: string): number {
   113→  const match = interval.trim().toLowerCase().match(/^(\d+)\s*(s|m|h)$/)
   114→  if (!match) {
   115→    return intervalToSeconds(DEFAULT_HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL)
   116→  }
   117→  const value = parseInt(match[1]!, 10)
   118→  const unit = match[2]!
   119→  if (value <= 0) {
   120→    return intervalToSeconds(DEFAULT_HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL)
   121→  }
   122→  const multiplier: Record<string, number> = { s: 1, m: 60, h: 3600 }
   123→  return value * (multiplier[unit] ?? 60)
   124→}
   125→
   126→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   127→// Project registration
   128→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   129→
   130→export function registerProject(cfg: Config, repoPath: string): Config {
   131→  const resolved = toPosixPath(resolve(repoPath))
   132→  for (const entry of cfg.projects) {
   133→    if (toPosixPath(resolve(entry.path)) === resolved) {
   134→      return cfg
   135→    }
   136→  }
   137→  const name = resolved.split('/').pop() ?? ''
   138→  return {
   139→    ...cfg,
   140→    projects: [...cfg.projects, { path: resolved, name }],
   141→  }
   142→}
   143→
   144→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   145→// Internal helpers
   146→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   147→
   148→function defaultConfig(): Config {
   149→  return {
   150→    heartbeat_interval: DEFAULT_HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL,
   151→    watch_dirs: [],
   152→    projects: [],
   153→    auto_push: DEFAULT_AUTO_PUSH,
   154→    log_level: DEFAULT_LOG_LEVEL,
   155→    generate_report: false,
   156→    locale: 'en',
   157→    ai_summary_model: 'claude-haiku-4-5-20251001',
   158→    gh_pages_branch: '',
   159→    cname: '',
   160→    webhook_url: '',
   161→    report_base_url: '',
   162→  }
   163→}
   164→
   165→function parseRaw(raw: Record<string, unknown>): Config {
   166→  let interval = raw['heartbeat_interval']
   167→  if (typeof interval !== 'string' || !isValidInterval(interval)) {
   168→    interval = DEFAULT_HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL
   169→  }
   170→
   171→  let autoPush = raw['auto_push']
   172→  if (typeof autoPush !== 'boolean') {
   173→    autoPush = DEFAULT_AUTO_PUSH
   174→  }
   175→
   176→  let logLevel = raw['log_level']
   177→  if (typeof logLevel !== 'string' || !VALID_LOG_LEVELS.has(logLevel.toLowerCase())) {
   178→    logLevel = DEFAULT_LOG_LEVEL
   179→  }
   180→  logLevel = (logLevel as string).toLowerCase()
   181→
   182→  const watchDirs: string[] = []
   183→  const rawDirs = raw['watch_dirs']
   184→  if (Array.isArray(rawDirs)) {
   185→    for (const d of rawDirs) {
   186→      if (typeof d === 'string') watchDirs.push(d)
   187→    }
   188→  }
   189→
   190→  const projects: ProjectEntry[] = []
   191→  const rawProjects = raw['projects']
   192→  if (Array.isArray(rawProjects)) {
   193→    for (const entry of rawProjects) {
   194→      if (entry !== null && typeof entry === 'object' && !Array.isArray(entry)) {
   195→        const rec = entry as Record<string, unknown>
   196→        if (typeof rec['path'] === 'string') {
   197→          projects.push({
   198→            path: rec['path'] as string,
   199→            name: String(rec['name'] ?? ''),
   200→          })
   201→        }
   202→      }
   203→    }
   204→  }
   205→
   206→  return {
   207→    heartbeat_interval: interval as string,
   208→    watch_dirs: watchDirs,
   209→    projects,
   210→    auto_push: autoPush as boolean,
   211→    log_level: logLevel as string,
   212→    generate_report: typeof raw['generate_report'] === 'boolean' ? raw['generate_report'] : false,
   213→    locale: typeof raw['locale'] === 'string' ? raw['locale'] : 'en',
   214→    ai_summary_model: typeof raw['ai_summary_model'] === 'string' ? raw['ai_summary_model'] : 'claude-haiku-4-5-20251001',
   215→    gh_pages_branch: typeof raw['gh_pages_branch'] === 'string' ? raw['gh_pages_branch'] : '',
   216→    cname: typeof raw['cname'] === 'string' ? raw['cname'] : '',
   217→    webhook_url: typeof raw['webhook_url'] === 'string' ? raw['webhook_url'] : '',
   218→    report_base_url: typeof raw['report_base_url'] === 'string' ? raw['report_base_url'] : '',
   219→  }
   220→}
   221→
   222→function isValidInterval(value: string): boolean {
   223→  const match = value.trim().toLowerCase().match(/^(\d+)\s*(s|m|h)$/)
   224→  if (!match) return false
   225→  return parseInt(match[1]!, 10) > 0
   226→}
   227→
   228→function tomlString(value: string): string {
   229→  const escaped = value.replace(/\\/g, '\\\\').replace(/"/g, '\\"')
   230→  return `"${escaped}"`
   231→}
   232→

{

  "content": "import type { ManifestEntry } from '@openmnemo/types'\nimport type { SearchResult } from '../../transcript/db.js'\nimport { upsertSearchIndex, searchTranscripts } from '../../transcript/db.js'\nimport type { SearchAdapter, SearchOptions } from './search-adapter.js'\n\nexport class SqliteFtsAdapter implements SearchAdapter {\n  constructor(private readonly dbPath: string) {}\n\n  search(query: string, options: SearchOptions = {}): SearchResult[] {\n    return searchTranscripts(this.dbPath, query, options.limit ?? 20)\n  }\n\n  upsert(manifest: ManifestEntry): void {\n    upsertSearchIndex(this.dbPath, manifest)\n  }\n\n  close(): void {\n    // stateless — db is opened/closed per-call in db.ts\n  }\n}\n"
}

{
  "file_path": "D:\\demo1\\openmnemo\\packages\\core\\src\\storage\\factory.ts",
  "content": "import { join } from 'node:path'\nimport { SqliteFtsAdapter } from './search/sqlite-fts-adapter.js'\nimport { QdrantVectorAdapter } from './vector/qdrant-adapter.js'\nimport { Neo4jGraphAdapter } from './graph/neo4j-adapter.js'\nimport type { SearchAdapter } from './search/search-adapter.js'\nimport type { VectorAdapter } from './vector/vector-adapter.js'\nimport type { GraphAdapter } from './graph/graph-adapter.js'\n\nexport interface StorageConfig {\n  indexDir: string\n  vector_backend?: 'sqlite-vec' | 'qdrant'\n  graph_backend?: 'sqlite' | 'neo4j'\n  qdrant_url?: string\n  qdrant_collection?: string\n  neo4j_uri?: string\n  neo4j_user?: string\n  neo4j_password?: string\n}\n\nexport function createSearchAdapter(cfg: StorageConfig): SearchAdapter {\n  return new SqliteFtsAdapter(join(cfg.indexDir, 'search.sqlite'))\n}\n\nexport function createVectorAdapter(cfg: StorageConfig): VectorAdapter {\n  if (cfg.vector_backend === 'qdrant') {\n    return new QdrantVectorAdapter(\n      cfg.qdrant_url ?? 'http://localhost:6333',\n      cfg.qdrant_collection ?? 'transcripts',\n    )\n  }\n  throw new Error('sqlite-vec VectorAdapter not implemented in Phase 1')\n}\n\nexport function createGraphAdapter(cfg: StorageConfig): GraphAdapter {\n  if (cfg.graph_backend === 'neo4j') {\n    return new Neo4jGraphAdapter(\n      cfg.neo4j_uri ?? 'bolt://localhost:7687',\n      cfg.neo4j_user ?? 'neo4j',\n      cfg.neo4j_password ?? '',\n    )\n  }\n  throw new Error('sqlite GraphAdapter not implemented in Phase 1')\n}\n"
}
